document.title: TkVillage app.py Data Dictionary
document.purpose: Document the runtime data owned by src/tkvillage/app.py using SoftSpec v3, including structural schemas and data-dictionary annotations for runtime ownership, lifecycle, reset behavior, and serialization boundaries.
author: Codex

orientation.summary: TkVillage keeps runtime state in src/tkvillage/app.py. Scalar runtime values live in the module-level g dictionary. Mutable runtime containers such as registries, queues, logs, and maps live as explicit module-level dictionaries or lists. reset_app() preserves the identity of these containers by clearing and updating them rather than rebinding them.
runtime-style: registry and log containers exposed as named globals
runtime-only Python objects isolated in runtime records or runtime_objects

notes: This SoftSpec v3 document describes live Python runtime state, not a persisted JSON file format.
Ordinary SoftSpec fields describe value structure, meaning, and JSON representability.
The data-dictionary annotations describe ownership, mutation, lifecycle, reset behavior, runtime-only boundaries, export behavior, and debug visibility.
Container identity matters: reset_app() clears and updates existing dictionaries and lists instead of rebinding them.

data-dictionary.reset_policy: reset_app() preserves module-level container identities by clearing dictionaries and lists, then repopulating g and debug with default scalar fields.
data-dictionary.persistence: runtime-only except for config_values when save_config() writes JSON-compatible values to config.json.
rules: Reducer-owned semantic state should remain JSON-clean where practical.
Live Tk objects and Python function references should stay in runtime records or runtime_objects, not in reducer-owned semantic state.
Bounded logs are append-only in normal use and trimmed by append_log().

python-runtime-object.description: A live Python object that should remain outside JSON-clean semantic state.

function-reference.description: A Python callable stored in runtime registries for lifecycle, reducer, projection, message, or service behavior.

runtime-id.description: Readable runtime identifier generated or assigned by TkVillage.

typed-config-value.description: Current value of a configuration key after declaration and coercion where possible.
notes: Path config values are stored as pathlib.Path objects in memory and serialized as strings by save_config().

window-kind.description: Stable symbolic name for a registered window kind.

window-id.description: Unique identifier for one live window record.

instance-key.description: Lookup key used to summon or reuse a window instance.

target-id.description: Identifier of a routable runtime target such as a window or service.

runtime-object-id.description: Identifier for an object stored in runtime_objects.

worker-id.description: Reserved identifier for a future worker-thread record.

id-prefix.description: Prefix consumed by next_id(prefix) when generating readable runtime ids.

unix-timestamp-seconds.description: Seconds since the Unix epoch as returned by time.time().

window-multiplicity.description: Summon and reuse policy declared by a window kind.

vocabulary.singleton: One instance exists; summon raises the existing window.
vocabulary.per-key: One instance exists per caller-provided key.
vocabulary.free-instance: Every summon creates a fresh instance.
vocabulary.embedded: Declared but not implemented in v1.
vocabulary.modal: Declared but not implemented in v1.

effect.description: Explicit request for runtime work outside a reducer.

app-module-runtime-state.title: app.py Runtime State
app-module-runtime-state.description: The complete module-level runtime data surface owned by src/tkvillage/app.py.
rules: Scalar runtime values live inside g.
Mutable registries, queues, logs, and maps live as explicit module-level dictionaries or lists.
reset_app() preserves container identity by clearing and updating existing containers.
Runtime-only Python objects should not be inserted into reducer-owned semantic state.

data-dictionary.created_by: module import initializes empty containers; reset_app() populates defaults
cleared_by: shutdown() for selected live Tk resources
data-dictionary.reset_policy: Container identities are preserved across reset_app(); contents are cleared and selected dictionaries are repopulated.

g.description: Small global dictionary for scalar runtime values and root/project handles.

data-dictionary.identity_policy: Preserved across reset_app() with clear() and update().
data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared and repopulated with default scalar values by reset_app().
data-dictionary.used_for: Runtime identity, Tk root handle, project directory, tick loop settings, and log bounds.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Debug/export tools should convert runtime-only values such as root and project_dir to summaries or strings.

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared by reset_app(); repopulated by declare_config() calls.
data-dictionary.used_for: Configuration declaration metadata consumed by load_config(), set_config(), and list_config().
data-dictionary.export_behavior: May be shown directly in debug views; not itself persisted by save_config().

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared by reset_app(); populated by declare_config(), load_config(), and set_config().
data-dictionary.used_for: Runtime configuration lookup and persistence through config.json.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: save_config() serializes Path values as strings before writing config.json.

window_kinds.description: Registry of window kind records that describe summon policy and lifecycle functions.

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared by reset_app(); populated by register_window_kind() and register_debug_windows().
data-dictionary.used_for: Summoning, creating, reducing, projecting, and lifecycle handling for window records.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Debug snapshots include only JSON-friendly summaries such as title, multiplicity, and debug_label.

windows.description: Registry of live window records by window_id.

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared by reset_app(); individual windows are removed by destroy_window().
data-dictionary.used_for: Live window lookup, event routing, projection, lifecycle hooks, and debug summaries.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Debug snapshots should summarize live records and avoid serializing Tk objects or widget references.

windows_by_instance_key.description: Lookup table from summon instance_key to live window_id.

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared by reset_app(); entries are added by realize_window() and removed by destroy_window().
data-dictionary.used_for: Reusing singleton and per-key windows when summoned again.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: May be included in debug output as a string-to-string map.

targets.description: Registry of queued runtime targets, including windows and non-window services.

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared by reset_app(); window targets are added by realize_window() and removed by destroy_window(); service targets are added by register_service_target().
data-dictionary.used_for: Queueing and dispatching events/messages to windows and services.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Debug output should avoid serializing handler callables and live inbox aliases directly.

runtime_objects.description: Optional registry for Python objects that should stay outside semantic state.

data-dictionary.used_for: Parking live Python objects away from JSON-clean semantic state.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Do not serialize values; export only ids and summaries if needed.

event_log.description: Bounded log of recently posted runtime events.

data-dictionary.used_for: Debugging recent post_event() calls.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Included in debug snapshots as recent_events; bounded by g.max_log_entries.

effect_log.description: Bounded log of recently routed effects.

data-dictionary.used_for: Debugging route_effect() and route_effects() behavior.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Included in debug snapshots as recent_effects; bounded by g.max_log_entries.

message_log.description: Bounded log of recently sent runtime messages.

data-dictionary.used_for: Debugging send_message() calls and message envelopes.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Included in debug snapshots as recent_messages; bounded by g.max_log_entries.

runtime_log.description: Bounded log of runtime notes, unknown effects, and tick exceptions.

data-dictionary.used_for: Debugging unknown effects, runtime notes, and tick failures.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Included in debug snapshots as runtime_log; bounded by g.max_log_entries.

worker_records.description: Reserved registry for future worker-thread records.

data-dictionary.used_for: Reserved for future worker-thread integration.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: No current export behavior; future worker objects should not be serialized directly.

debug.description: Small runtime dictionary for debug mode and debug snapshot state.

data-dictionary.identity_policy: Preserved across reset_app() with clear() and update().
data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared and repopulated by reset_app() with enabled=false and last_snapshot=null.
data-dictionary.used_for: Debug mode flag and cached last debug snapshot.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: last_snapshot is itself debug/export material and may contain summarized runtime state.

id_counters.description: Counter map used by next_id(prefix) to generate stable readable runtime identifiers.

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: Cleared by reset_app(); incremented by next_id(prefix).
data-dictionary.used_for: Generating readable runtime ids such as window-1 and instance-1.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: May be shown in debug output if useful; not persisted.

g-runtime-state.title: g Runtime State
g-runtime-state.description: Scalar runtime values and root/project handles stored in app.g.

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: reset_app() clears g and repopulates all listed keys.
data-dictionary.identity_policy: The dictionary identity is preserved; individual field values are replaced during reset.
data-dictionary.persistence: runtime-only; project_dir points to where config persistence occurs.

name.description: Current TkVillage application name used for runtime identity and debug snapshots.

root.description: The hidden Tk root that anchors the Tk interpreter, event loop, after scheduling, bindings, and clipboard access.

data-dictionary.lifecycle: Created lazily when needed; destroyed during reset or shutdown.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Do not serialize. Represent only by a debug summary if needed.

project_dir.description: Resolved directory used for project-local runtime data such as config.json.

data-dictionary.lifecycle: Resolved during declare_app(); cleared during reset.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Convert to string when represented in JSON or debug output.

is_running.description: Whether the runtime should be considered active and eligible to schedule ticks.

data-dictionary.used_for: Controls tick scheduling eligibility.

test_mode.description: Whether the runtime is executing under test-oriented behavior.

data-dictionary.used_for: tick_once() re-raises tick exceptions when test_mode is true.

strict_effects.description: Whether unknown effects should raise an exception instead of being logged.

data-dictionary.used_for: Controls route_effect() behavior for unknown effect types.

tick_interval_ms.description: Delay between scheduled tick loop iterations.

data-dictionary.used_for: schedule_next_tick() delay.

tick_after_id.description: Tk after() handle for the next scheduled tick loop callback.

data-dictionary.lifecycle: Set when the tick loop schedules a callback; cleared when the loop is stopped or reset.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Do not serialize.

tick_count.description: Number of completed runtime ticks since the last reset_app().

data-dictionary.used_for: Runtime progress/debug counter.

created_at.description: Timestamp marking when the current runtime state was initialized.

data-dictionary.lifecycle: Replaced each time runtime state is reset.

max_log_entries.description: Maximum number of entries retained in bounded runtime logs.

data-dictionary.used_for: append_log() trims event_log, effect_log, message_log, and runtime_log to this size.
maybe_begin_shutdown() policy decision at tick end.

shutdown_window_kind.description: Window kind watched by the on-window-close shutdown policy.

data-dictionary.used_for: maybe_begin_shutdown() checks whether this kind has closed.

shutdown_requested.description: Explicit shutdown request flag set by request_shutdown().

data-dictionary.used_for: explicit shutdown policy.
Debugging/logging the explicit shutdown request.

is_shutting_down.description: Whether graceful or hard shutdown has begun.

data-dictionary.used_for: Prevents duplicate graceful shutdown starts.

shutdown_handler.description: Optional no-argument callable invoked once when shutdown begins.

data-dictionary.used_for: User-supplied shutdown procedure.

shutdown_handler_called.description: Whether shutdown_handler has already been invoked.

data-dictionary.used_for: Ensures the shutdown handler runs at most once.

has_had_window.description: Whether any window has been realized since reset.

data-dictionary.used_for: Prevents on-last-window-close from firing before the first window is summoned.

shutdown_window_kind_seen.description: Whether a window of shutdown_window_kind has been realized since reset.

data-dictionary.used_for: Prevents on-window-close from firing before the watched window kind is summoned.

debug-runtime-state.title: Debug Runtime State
debug-runtime-state.description: Small dictionary storing debug mode and the latest debug snapshot.

data-dictionary.reset_behavior: reset_app() clears debug and sets enabled=false and last_snapshot=null.
data-dictionary.identity_policy: The dictionary identity is preserved across reset_app().

enabled.description: Whether debug mode is currently enabled.

data-dictionary.used_for: Controls whether the debug dashboard is summoned.

last_snapshot.description: Most recent debug snapshot produced by get_debug_snapshot().

data-dictionary.used_for: Caches the latest generated debug snapshot.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Designed for JSON output via json.dumps(..., default=str).

config-declaration-record.title: Config Declaration Record
config-declaration-record.description: Record stored in config_declarations by declare_config().

data-dictionary.used_for: Type, default, description, and choices metadata for configuration keys.

default.description: Default value for this configuration key.

description.description: Human-readable explanation of the configuration key.

choices.description: Allowed choice values when type is choice; otherwise an empty list.

window-kind-record.title: Window Kind Record
window-kind-record.description: Record stored in window_kinds by register_window_kind().

data-dictionary.used_for: Defines how windows of this kind are summoned, created, reduced, projected, and handled during lifecycle transitions.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Debug snapshots include only title, multiplicity, and debug_label.

multiplicity.description: Summon policy for this window kind.

create.description: Function that builds the Tk widgets for a realized window.

make_initial_state.description: Function that creates the reducer-owned initial state.

project.description: Projection function that updates widgets from state.

on_show.description: Optional hook called when a window is raised.

on_close.description: Optional hook called before a window is destroyed through close_window().

on_destroy.description: Optional hook called while destroying a window.

on_tick.description: Optional hook called during tick processing.

window-record.title: Window Record
window-record.description: Live window record stored in windows by realize_window().

data-dictionary.lifecycle: Created when a window is realized; removed from windows when destroyed; associated target and instance-key entries are removed at the same time.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Debug snapshots use summarize_window() rather than serializing this record directly.

target_id.description: Same identifier as window_id for routable window targets.

multiplicity.description: Window multiplicity copied from the kind record.

key.description: Optional per-key object identity supplied by caller.

payload.description: Optional summon payload supplied by caller.

toplevel.description: Live Tk toplevel object for the window.

data-dictionary.export_behavior: Do not serialize. Represent only by debug label or object summary.

state.description: Reducer-owned semantic state dictionary for this window.

data-dictionary.owned_by: window reducer for this window kind
data-dictionary.export_behavior: May appear in debug snapshots; should avoid live Python objects.

event_queue.description: List of queued semantic events for this window.

data-dictionary.lifecycle: Aliased by the corresponding target-record inbox.

data-dictionary.export_behavior: Do not serialize widget objects.

last_shown_at.description: Timestamp of most recent raise_window() call, or null before first raise.

is_open.description: Whether the window is considered open.

is_destroyed.description: Whether destroy_window() has marked this record destroyed.

needs_project.description: Whether project_window() should run for this window.

target-record.title: Target Record
target-record.description: Queued runtime target record stored in targets.

data-dictionary.used_for: Runtime event/message routing.

data-dictionary.lifecycle: For window targets this is the same list object as window-record.event_queue.

handler.description: Optional service handler called during tick processing.

is_active.description: Whether the target should receive queued work.

log-entry.title: Log Entry
log-entry.description: General shape used by event_log, effect_log, message_log, and runtime_log.

data-dictionary.lifecycle: Appended during runtime and trimmed to g.max_log_entries.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Included in debug snapshots; values should stay JSON-friendly where practical.

target_id.description: Optional target id associated with the entry.

source_id.description: Optional source id associated with the entry.

message.description: Optional runtime message, log text, or message envelope.

worker-record.title: Worker Record
worker-record.description: Reserved future worker-thread record stored in worker_records.

data-dictionary.lifecycle: Reserved; no current writer beyond reset_app() clearing the registry.
data-dictionary.export_behavior: Future worker thread objects should not be serialized directly.
